seoTitle: Burma Road vs Burnside — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Burma Road and Burnside. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Burma Road vs Burnside: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burma Road and Burnside.
heading: Burma Road vs Burnside — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burma Road, Western Australia and Burnside, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burnside has a lower overall climate risk score (24/100) compared to Burma Road (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burma Road or Burnside safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burma Road has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Burnside scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burma Road scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Burnside scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burma Road or Burnside?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burnside has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.77% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
